title: How Much Does It Cost to Start a Landscaping Business in Georgia?
content:
Starting a Landscaping Business in Georgia typically costs between $4,600 and $46,000. That is about 8% lower than the national average of $5,000-$50,000, driven primarily by Georgia's below-average cost of living, labor rates, and commercial rents.

Georgia's combination of low costs, business-friendly regulations, and Atlanta's status as a major economic hub makes it attractive for startups. The state offers generous tax credits for small businesses and has invested heavily in infrastructure. Atlanta's Hartsfield-Jackson airport makes the city a natural logistics and distribution center.

From a regulatory standpoint, Georgia has specific requirements to plan for. Georgia levies a moderate state income tax of up to 5.5%, which is a factor in your long-term profitability planning. Georgia follows the federal minimum wage of $7.25/hour, though market rates for skilled workers are typically much higher.

Cost Breakdown for Georgia

Costs adjusted for Georgia's cost of living, labor rates, and commercial rents.

Georgia Business Requirements

To legally operate a landscaping business in Georgia, you will need to:

  • Form an LLC or business entity - The filing fee in Georgia is $100, with a $50 annual report fee.
  • Obtain a business license - Requirements and fees vary by city. Contact your local Atlanta or Savannah clerk's office for specifics.
  • Register for sales tax - Georgia's state sales tax rate is 4%. Local additions can push the effective rate higher. You will need a sales tax permit if you sell taxable goods or services.
  • Plan for state income tax - Georgia's top rate is 5.5%. Set aside a portion of profits for quarterly estimated payments.
  • Get business insurance - General liability insurance is essential in Georgia. Most landlords and clients require at least $1 million in coverage.

Why Georgia Costs Differ

Georgia's cost of living is 7% below the national average, which reduces the cost of supplies, services, and day-to-day expenses. Labor costs in Georgia are near the national average. Commercial rents in Georgia are slightly below the national average, with significant variation between Atlanta and smaller cities.

Georgia-Specific Tips

  • Georgia's warm climate means a longer operating season than northern states, which helps you recoup startup costs faster - but summer heat requires hydration planning and earlier start times for crews.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does it cost to start a landscaping business in Georgia?

Starting a landscaping business in Georgia typically costs between $4,600 and $46,000, depending on your location within the state, your business model, and how lean you launch. Costs in Atlanta tend to run higher than in smaller Georgia cities like Columbus.

Do I need a special license to operate a landscaping business in Georgia?

Yes. At minimum, you need a Georgia business license and any industry-specific permits required by your city or county. LLC formation costs $100 in Georgia, plus a $50 annual report fee. Check with your local Atlanta or Savannah city hall for specific requirements.

How does Georgia's state income tax affect my landscaping business?

Georgia's top state income tax rate is 5.5%. As a business owner, you will typically pay this on your business profits through your personal return (if you are an LLC or sole proprietorship). Factor this into your pricing and profitability projections from the start.

Is Georgia a good state to start a landscaping business?

Georgia offers below-average costs that help new businesses reach profitability faster. Georgia's combination of low costs, business-friendly regulations, and Atlanta's status as a major economic hub makes it attractive for startups.

See our full national Landscaping Business cost guide for detailed breakdowns, hidden costs, and money-saving strategies that apply everywhere.
